sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.93 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (M.\,Herzog, J.\,Sch\"onheim). Let$G$be a group and let$k 2$. Let$H_1, ,H_k$be subgroups of$G$, and$g_1, ,g_k$elements of$G$such that the cosets$g_1H_1, ,g_kH_k$form a partition of$G$. Is it true that$|G:H_i| = |G:H_j|$for some$i j$? This is known to be true for groups with a Sylow tower (M.\,A.\,Berger, A.\,Felzen\-baum, A.\,Fraenkel, Fund. Math., { 128}, no.\,3 (1987), 139--144). Also cf. 20.99.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
